svg.title: Vela kiosk wall: the sustained fullscreen chrome and the honest per-platform pinning story
svg.desc: On the left, the wall lives edge to edge: a landscape frame with no OS status bar, no home indicator, and no app header or nav. The background paints to the very edge while content sits inside the safe-area insets, and the only affordance is the invisible exit corner. On the right, a panel states honestly that pinning is best-effort, not a lock. Three rows: Android and Fire user screen pinning blocks a casual tap but the owner can leave, marked best-effort; Android device-owner lock task is a true lock but needs provisioning; iOS Guided Access is user-initiated with no App Store API to force it. A conclusion states that the app-level gesture and PIN exit is the portable guard because it is enforced in the app, not the OS, and pinning is a convenience layered on top. A footer states AOD-11 section 9 owns the pinning mechanics and honesty, AOD-21 section 9 owns the entry transition, and this design fixes only the sustained fullscreen presentation and does not redraw the entry.
svg.text: How the wall lives once entered: edge to edge, no OS chrome, an invisible lock. Pinning is honest about what it can enforce.
no status bar · no home indicator · no header / nav
background paints to the edge; content sits in the safe insets
the only affordance is the invisible exit corner (bottom-right)
AOD-11 §9 · what a consumer install can enforce
A casual tap is blocked. The owner can still leave via Back +
Overview, so the app gesture + PIN is the real guard.
No exit until stopLockTask(). Needs provisioning (ADB dpm or
MDM) on a clean device. The power-user lockdown, not consumer.
The user triple-clicks to start it. There is no App Store API to
force it on an unsupervised device; the app surfaces guidance.

text.tspan: The app gesture + PIN is the portable guard.

svg.text: It holds on every platform because it is enforced in the app, not
the OS. Pinning is a convenience layered on top, never the lock.
AOD-11 §9 owns the pinning mechanics; AOD-21 §9 owns the entry transition. This fixes the sustained fullscreen presentation, not the entry.
the entry (push fullscreen, chrome hidden) is AOD-21 §9 · here the wall LIVES pinned: edge-to-edge, no controls, exit only by the corner + PIN
